verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface during epididymal transit. This binding is stable and wash-resistant — CRISP1 is deposited onto the sperm plasma membrane in the epididymal lumen and remains stably associated with the postacrosomal/equatorial segment through fertilization. This constitutes a stable post-translational surface attachment to a cell surface rather than a transient equilibrium with a soluble pool. The protein is thus surface-accessible on sperm cells via this mechanism, qualifying as contextual/stable_surface_attachment in a tissue-restricted (epididymis/sperm) context.
